title: × Rhynchanthe bernardii
taxonomy: × Rhynchanthe bernardii is a natural hybrid species. It belongs to the family Begoniaceae. The classification of × Rhynchanthe bernardii includes being a member of the genus Rhynchanthe, with two parent species. The parent species of × Rhynchanthe bernardii are Rhynchanthe grandiflora and Begonia coccinea. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: × Rhynchanthe bernardii, also known as Bernard's rhynchanthe, is a sterile, hybrid species of flowering plant, specifically a cross between Rhynchospora cassyroides and Rhynchospora bernardii. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of × Rhynchanthe bernardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, × Rhynchanthe bernardii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be found in various commercial products such as cough syrups and expectorants.
